commands.overview:
Codex CLI

If no subcommand is specified, options will be forwarded to the interactive CLI.

subcommands.name: exec
subcommands.description: Run Codex non-interactively

subcommands.name: review
subcommands.description: Run a code review non-interactively

subcommands.name: login
subcommands.description: Manage login

subcommands.name: logout
subcommands.description: Remove stored authentication credentials

subcommands.name: mcp
subcommands.description: Manage external MCP servers for Codex

subcommands.name: mcp-server
subcommands.description: Start Codex as an MCP server (stdio)

subcommands.name: app-server
subcommands.description: [experimental] Run the app server or related tooling

subcommands.name: completion
subcommands.description: Generate shell completion scripts

subcommands.name: sandbox
subcommands.description: Run commands within a Codex-provided sandbox

subcommands.name: debug
subcommands.description: Debugging tools

subcommands.name: apply
subcommands.description: Apply the latest diff produced by Codex agent as a `git apply` to your local working tree

subcommands.name: resume
subcommands.description: Resume a previous interactive session (picker by default; use --last to continue the most recent)

subcommands.name: fork
subcommands.description: Fork a previous interactive session (picker by default; use --last to fork the most recent)

subcommands.name: cloud
subcommands.description: [EXPERIMENTAL] Browse tasks from Codex Cloud and apply changes locally

subcommands.name: features
subcommands.description: Inspect feature flags

options.description: Override a configuration value that would otherwise be loaded from `~/.codex/config.toml`. Use a dotted path (`foo.bar.baz`) to override nested values. The `value` portion is parsed as TOML. If it fails to parse as TOML, the raw string is used as a literal. Examples: - `-c model="o3"` - `-c 'sandbox_permissions=["disk-full-read-access"]'` - `-c shell_environment_policy.inherit=all`
Enable a feature (repeatable). Equivalent to `-c features.<name>=true`
Disable a feature (repeatable). Equivalent to `-c features.<name>=false`
Connect the app-server-backed TUI to a remote app server websocket endpoint. Accepted forms: `ws://host:port` or `wss://host:port`.
Optional image(s) to attach to the initial prompt
Model the agent should use
Convenience flag to select the local open source model provider. Equivalent to -c model_provider=oss; verifies a local LM Studio or Ollama server is running
Specify which local provider to use (lmstudio or ollama). If not specified with --oss, will use config default or show selection
Configuration profile from config.toml to specify default options
Select the sandbox policy to use when executing model-generated shell commands [possible values: read-only, workspace-write, danger-full-access]

Configure when the model requires human approval before executing a command Possible values: - untrusted:  Only run "trusted" commands (e.g. ls, cat, sed) without asking for user approval. Will escalate to the user if the model proposes a command that is not in the "trusted" set - on-failure: DEPRECATED: Run all commands without asking for user approval. Only asks for approval if a command fails to execute, in which case it will escalate to the user to ask for un-sandboxed execution. Prefer `on-request` for interactive runs or `never` for non-interactive runs - on-request: The model decides when to ask the user for approval - never:      Never ask for user approval Execution failures are immediately returned to the model
Convenience alias for low-friction sandboxed automatic execution (-a on-request, --sandbox workspace-write)
Skip all confirmation prompts and execute commands without sandboxing. EXTREMELY DANGEROUS. Intended solely for running in environments that are externally sandboxed
Tell the agent to use the specified directory as its working root
Enable live web search. When enabled, the native Responses `web_search` tool is available to the model (no per‑call approval)
Additional directories that should be writable alongside the primary workspace

Disable alternate screen mode Runs the TUI in inline mode, preserving terminal scrollback history. This is useful in terminal multiplexers like Zellij that follow the xterm spec strictly and disable scrollback in alternate screen buffers.
Print help (see a summary with '-h')
Print version

commands.overview: [experimental] Run the app server or related tooling

subcommands.name: generate-ts
subcommands.description: [experimental] Generate TypeScript bindings for the app server protocol

subcommands.name: generate-json-schema
subcommands.description: [experimental] Generate JSON Schema for the app server protocol

options.description: Override a configuration value that would otherwise be loaded from `~/.codex/config.toml`. Use a dotted path (`foo.bar.baz`) to override nested values. The `value` portion is parsed as TOML. If it fails to parse as TOML, the raw string is used as a literal. Examples: - `-c model="o3"` - `-c 'sandbox_permissions=["disk-full-read-access"]'` - `-c shell_environment_policy.inherit=all`
Enable a feature (repeatable). Equivalent to `-c features.<name>=true`
Disable a feature (repeatable). Equivalent to `-c features.<name>=false`
Transport endpoint URL. Supported values: `stdio://` (default), `ws://IP:PORT` [default: stdio://]

Session source stamped into new threads started by this app-server. Known values such as `vscode`, `cli`, `exec`, and `mcp` map to built-in sources. Any other non-empty value is recorded as a custom source. [default: vscode]

Controls whether analytics are enabled by default. Analytics are disabled by default for app-server. Users have to explicitly opt in via the `analytics` section in the config.toml file. However, for first-party use cases like the VSCode IDE extension, we default analytics to be enabled by default by setting this flag. Users can still opt out by setting this in their config.toml: ```toml [analytics] enabled = false ``` See https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-advanced/#metrics for more details.
Print help (see a summary with '-h')

commands.overview: [experimental] Generate JSON Schema for the app server protocol

options.description: Override a configuration value that would otherwise be loaded from `~/.codex/config.toml`. Use a dotted path (`foo.bar.baz`) to override nested values. Examples: - `-c model="o3"` - `-c 'sandbox_permissions=["disk-full-read-access"]'` - `-c shell_environment_policy.inherit=all`
Output directory where the schema bundle will be written

Include experimental methods and fields in the generated output
Disable a feature (repeatable). Equivalent to `-c features.<name>=false`
Print help (see a summary with '-h')

Output directory where .ts files will be written

Optional path to the Prettier executable to format generated files
Disable a feature (repeatable). Equivalent to `-c features.<name>=false`
Include experimental methods and fields in the generated output

commands.overview: [EXPERIMENTAL] Browse tasks from Codex Cloud and apply changes locally

commands.overview: Run Codex non-interactively

subcommands.name: resume
subcommands.description: Resume a previous session by id or pick the most recent with --last

subcommands.name: review
subcommands.description: Run a code review against the current repository

options.description: Override a configuration value that would otherwise be loaded from `~/.codex/config.toml`. Use a dotted path (`foo.bar.baz`) to override nested values. The `value` portion is parsed as TOML. If it fails to parse as TOML, the raw string is used as a literal. Examples: - `-c model="o3"` - `-c 'sandbox_permissions=["disk-full-read-access"]'` - `-c shell_environment_policy.inherit=all`
Enable a feature (repeatable). Equivalent to `-c features.<name>=true`
Disable a feature (repeatable). Equivalent to `-c features.<name>=false`
Optional image(s) to attach to the initial prompt
Model the agent should use
Use open-source provider
Specify which local provider to use (lmstudio or ollama). If not specified with --oss, will use config default or show selection
Select the sandbox policy to use when executing model-generated shell commands [possible values: read-only, workspace-write, danger-full-access]
Configuration profile from config.toml to specify default options
Convenience alias for low-friction sandboxed automatic execution (-a on-request, --sandbox workspace-write)
Skip all confirmation prompts and execute commands without sandboxing. EXTREMELY DANGEROUS. Intended solely for running in environments that are externally sandboxed
Tell the agent to use the specified directory as its working root
Allow running Codex outside a Git repository
Additional directories that should be writable alongside the primary workspace
Run without persisting session files to disk
Path to a JSON Schema file describing the model's final response shape
Specifies color settings for use in the output [default: auto] [possible values: always, never, auto]
Force cursor-based progress updates in exec mode
Print events to stdout as JSONL
Specifies file where the last message from the agent should be written
Print help (see a summary with '-h')
Print version

commands.overview: Resume a previous session by id or pick the most recent with --last

options.description: Override a configuration value that would otherwise be loaded from `~/.codex/config.toml`. Use a dotted path (`foo.bar.baz`) to override nested values. The `value` portion is parsed as TOML. If it fails to parse as TOML, the raw string is used as a literal. Examples: - `-c model="o3"` - `-c 'sandbox_permissions=["disk-full-read-access"]'` - `-c shell_environment_policy.inherit=all`
Resume the most recent recorded session (newest) without specifying an id
Show all sessions (disables cwd filtering)
Enable a feature (repeatable). Equivalent to `-c features.<name>=true`
Disable a feature (repeatable). Equivalent to `-c features.<name>=false`
Optional image(s) to attach to the prompt sent after resuming
Model the agent should use
Convenience alias for low-friction sandboxed automatic execution (-a on-request, --sandbox workspace-write)
Skip all confirmation prompts and execute commands without sandboxing. EXTREMELY DANGEROUS. Intended solely for running in environments that are externally sandboxed
Allow running Codex outside a Git repository
Run without persisting session files to disk
Print events to stdout as JSONL
Specifies file where the last message from the agent should be written
Print help (see a summary with '-h')

commands.overview: Run a code review against the current repository

options.description: Override a configuration value that would otherwise be loaded from `~/.codex/config.toml`. Use a dotted path (`foo.bar.baz`) to override nested values. The `value` portion is parsed as TOML. If it fails to parse as TOML, the raw string is used as a literal. Examples: - `-c model="o3"` - `-c 'sandbox_permissions=["disk-full-read-access"]'` - `-c shell_environment_policy.inherit=all`
Review staged, unstaged, and untracked changes
Review changes against the given base branch
Enable a feature (repeatable). Equivalent to `-c features.<name>=true`
Review the changes introduced by a commit
Disable a feature (repeatable). Equivalent to `-c features.<name>=false`
Model the agent should use
Optional commit title to display in the review summary
Convenience alias for low-friction sandboxed automatic execution (-a on-request, --sandbox workspace-write)
Skip all confirmation prompts and execute commands without sandboxing. EXTREMELY DANGEROUS. Intended solely for running in environments that are externally sandboxed
Allow running Codex outside a Git repository
Run without persisting session files to disk
Print events to stdout as JSONL

options.description: Override a configuration value that would otherwise be loaded from `~/.codex/config.toml`. Use a dotted path (`foo.bar.baz`) to override nested values. The `value` portion is parsed as TOML. If it fails to parse as TOML, the raw string is used as a literal. Examples: - `-c model="o3"` - `-c 'sandbox_permissions=["disk-full-read-access"]'` - `-c shell_environment_policy.inherit=all`
Enable a feature (repeatable). Equivalent to `-c features.<name>=true`
Disable a feature (repeatable). Equivalent to `-c features.<name>=false`
Print help (see a summary with '-h')

commands.overview: Resume a previous interactive session (picker by default; use --last to continue the most recent)

options.description: Override a configuration value that would otherwise be loaded from `~/.codex/config.toml`. Use a dotted path (`foo.bar.baz`) to override nested values. The `value` portion is parsed as TOML. If it fails to parse as TOML, the raw string is used as a literal. Examples: - `-c model="o3"` - `-c 'sandbox_permissions=["disk-full-read-access"]'` - `-c shell_environment_policy.inherit=all`
Continue the most recent session without showing the picker
Show all sessions (disables cwd filtering and shows CWD column)
Enable a feature (repeatable). Equivalent to `-c features.<name>=true`
Disable a feature (repeatable). Equivalent to `-c features.<name>=false`
Connect the app-server-backed TUI to a remote app server websocket endpoint. Accepted forms: `ws://host:port` or `wss://host:port`.
Optional image(s) to attach to the initial prompt
Model the agent should use
Convenience flag to select the local open source model provider. Equivalent to -c model_provider=oss; verifies a local LM Studio or Ollama server is running
Specify which local provider to use (lmstudio or ollama). If not specified with --oss, will use config default or show selection
Configuration profile from config.toml to specify default options
Select the sandbox policy to use when executing model-generated shell commands [possible values: read-only, workspace-write, danger-full-access]

Configure when the model requires human approval before executing a command Possible values: - untrusted:  Only run "trusted" commands (e.g. ls, cat, sed) without asking for user approval. Will escalate to the user if the model proposes a command that is not in the "trusted" set - on-failure: DEPRECATED: Run all commands without asking for user approval. Only asks for approval if a command fails to execute, in which case it will escalate to the user to ask for un-sandboxed execution. Prefer `on-request` for interactive runs or `never` for non-interactive runs - on-request: The model decides when to ask the user for approval - never:      Never ask for user approval Execution failures are immediately returned to the model
Convenience alias for low-friction sandboxed automatic execution (-a on-request, --sandbox workspace-write)
Skip all confirmation prompts and execute commands without sandboxing. EXTREMELY DANGEROUS. Intended solely for running in environments that are externally sandboxed
Tell the agent to use the specified directory as its working root
Enable live web search. When enabled, the native Responses `web_search` tool is available to the model (no per‑call approval)
Additional directories that should be writable alongside the primary workspace

Disable alternate screen mode Runs the TUI in inline mode, preserving terminal scrollback history. This is useful in terminal multiplexers like Zellij that follow the xterm spec strictly and disable scrollback in alternate screen buffers.
Print help (see a summary with '-h')
Print version

commands.overview: Run a code review non-interactively

options.description: Override a configuration value that would otherwise be loaded from `~/.codex/config.toml`. Use a dotted path (`foo.bar.baz`) to override nested values. The `value` portion is parsed as TOML. If it fails to parse as TOML, the raw string is used as a literal. Examples: - `-c model="o3"` - `-c 'sandbox_permissions=["disk-full-read-access"]'` - `-c shell_environment_policy.inherit=all`
Review staged, unstaged, and untracked changes
Review changes against the given base branch
Enable a feature (repeatable). Equivalent to `-c features.<name>=true`
Review the changes introduced by a commit
Disable a feature (repeatable). Equivalent to `-c features.<name>=false`
Optional commit title to display in the review summary
Print help (see a summary with '-h')
